{{Quote|I prefer to make my credits gambling, but I never turn my back on an opportunity.|Gonto to [[Revan/Legends|Revan]]|<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref>}}
'''Gonto Yas''' was an elderly [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Pazaak/Legends|pazaak]] [[Gambling/Legends|gambler]] who [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] met in [[Ahto City]] in [[3956 BBY]]. When Revan asked him about the missing [[Selkath/Legends|Selkath]] he attempted to charge him two hundred [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for some information. He also revealed to Revan information about [[Sunry]]'s affair with [[Elassa Huros]] without charging (although he wanted to, he mistakenly uttered it). He said he wasn't interested in either the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] or the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]], and they weren't interested in him. When he heard about the [[Attack on Dantooine (Jedi Civil War)|attack]] on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] he openly mocked the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] defeat to Revan, not being aware that Revan was a Jedi.

==Personality and traits==
Gonto Yas was somewhat of a rude person and a sore loser when it came to pazaak. When he lost a game, he blamed it on luck, believing that his skill was better then others, even if he was on a losing streak. He liked to gossip, calling the affair between Sunry and Huros a "juicy tidbit." He was also in some ways a hustler who would try to earn credits by selling information that wasn't much of a help.

==Behind the scenes==
It is possible to make Yas reveal the information involving the missing Selkath without paying by threatening him. This gives the player [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] points. Performing a [[Mind trick/Legends|Force persuasion]] attempt on Yas also gives the player dark Side points.

If the player beats him ten times at pazaak, Yas will eventually refuse to play, saying that he can't afford to.
